priorities: 1) Testing & coverage → 2) Cross-platform trust-store discovery → 3) Distribution & installers
notes: Phase 1 (Foundation) and most of Phase 2 (platform trust stores, CI/release pipeline), Phase 3 (core features), and Phase 4 (advanced features) completed in 2026-Q1 — see docs/roadmaps/2026-Q1.md.
Pre-existing ROADMAP.md is retained read-only until this migration is verified.

sections.title: Platform Support & Distribution
sections.description: Installer/bundle outputs and per-OS build guides for end users.

sections.title: Trust Store Discovery
sections.description: Detect and surface non-standard / user-added root CAs without hard-coded paths or filesystem scanning. Driven by the discovery that homelab-ca was installed via update-ca-certificates but not surfaced because local_roots.json was stale.

tasks.description: Produce distributable .app bundle and .dmg for macOS.
steps: Bundle binary as .app
Package .app into .dmg

tasks.description: Produce a Windows installer for end users.

Produce installer in CI
acceptance_criteria: Installer runs on supported Windows versions

tasks.title: Linux: AppImage for distro-agnostic distribution
tasks.description: Build an AppImage so Linux users can run cert_viewer without per-distro packaging.
steps: Configure AppImage tooling
Produce AppImage in CI
acceptance_criteria: AppImage runs on Debian/Ubuntu and Fedora without extra dependencies

tasks.title: Cache freshness + standard env-var overrides for Linux trust store
tasks.description: Two small fixes that together would have surfaced the user's homelab CA today: (1) regenerate local_roots.json when the underlying bundle is newer than the cache, and (2) honor SSL_CERT_FILE / SSL_CERT_DIR env vars (the same overrides Go's crypto/x509 honors) so users who point their toolchain at a custom bundle get consistent behavior in cert_viewer. Today needsRegen() in internal/resources/localroots.go only triggers on legacy-format JSON, and localroots_linux.go hard-codes /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt.
steps: In internal/resources/localroots.go, change needsRegen() to also return true when bundle source mtime is newer than the cache mtime. Pass the source path through from collectRoots() so EnsureLocalRootsJSON can stat it.

In internal/resources/localroots_linux.go, before falling back to defaultLinuxBundle, check SSL_CERT_FILE; if set and readable, use it as the bundle source. If unset, check SSL_CERT_DIR (colon-separated) and concatenate PEMs from each directory.
Record the actual resolved source path in localRootsFile.SourcePath so the cache can be invalidated correctly when the env var changes between runs.

Add table-driven tests in localroots_linux_test.go covering: (a) cache regen when bundle mtime advances, (b) SSL_CERT_FILE override picked up, (c) SSL_CERT_DIR override with multiple .pem files merged, (d) precedence order SSL_CERT_FILE > SSL_CERT_DIR > default.
Update CLAUDE.md 'Runtime Paths' table to note that SSL_CERT_FILE / SSL_CERT_DIR override the default bundle.
acceptance_criteria: Touching /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt (or running update-ca-certificates) causes the next 'Compare Local vs CCADB' to regenerate the cache without manual deletion.
Setting SSL_CERT_FILE=/path/to/custom.pem before launch causes that bundle's roots to be used instead of the system default, and certs in it appear in 'Compare Local vs CCADB'.
Setting SSL_CERT_DIR=/dir1:/dir2 enumerates all *.pem / *.crt files across both directories.
Existing localroots_linux_test.go and localroots_test.go suites still pass; new tests cover the 4 cases above.
homelab CA (CN=pki, fingerprint 0B:A6:6E:43:F8:21:A3:AE:AF:9C:21:46:32:F9:98:06:DF:EA:7F:81:28:94:68:6D:8F:C9:4F:6F:EF:2B:58:C4) appears in local_roots.json on this workstation after the fix runs.
tasks.completion_summary: Added platform-specific resolveTrustSource()/trustSourceMTime(); Linux honors SSL_CERT_FILE then SSL_CERT_DIR (colon-separated dirs of *.pem/*.crt/*.cer) then defaults to /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt; cache regenerates when source identifier changes or source mtime exceeds cache mtime; macOS/Windows return ok=false for mtime (no single stat-able file). Verified locally: homelab-ca now appears in cache (147 roots vs 146 stale).

tasks.title: Trust Sources view: per-distro source dirs + per-user NSS DBs (Linux)
tasks.description: Move beyond reading a single concatenated bundle to a 'Trust Sources' view that enumerates each origin separately and labels each cert with where it came from. Linux-first scope: detect distro family via /etc/os-release, enumerate the appropriate user-added source directory (e.g. /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/ on Debian, /etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors/ on RHEL, /etc/ca-certificates/trust-source/anchors/ on Arch, /etc/pki/trust/anchors/ on SUSE), and read per-user NSS DBs (~/.pki/nssdb, ~/.mozilla/firefox/*/cert9.db). macOS keychain split (System vs login vs SystemRoots) and Windows CurrentUser+GPO are deliberately deferred to follow-up tasks under this section to keep scope tractable.
steps: Add internal/resources/distro_linux.go that parses /etc/os-release and returns a DistroFamily enum (debian, rhel, arch, suse, alpine, unknown) plus the matching anchor directory path.
Add internal/resources/anchors_linux.go that enumerates *.crt / *.pem files in the family-appropriate anchor directory and returns []TrustSourceEntry tagged with origin = 'distro-anchor-dir'.

Add internal/resources/nssdb_linux.go that locates ~/.pki/nssdb and ~/.mozilla/firefox/*/cert9.db and shells out to certutil -L -d sql:<path> if available, returning []TrustSourceEntry tagged with origin = 'nss-user' or 'nss-firefox'. Skip gracefully if certutil is not on PATH.

Extend LocalRootSummary with an Origin field and an OriginPath field; update writeLocalRoots() to merge entries from the system bundle, env-var overrides, anchor dir, and NSS DBs into one deduplicated list keyed by SHA-256 fingerprint.

Add a 'Trust Sources' tab in internal/ui/advanced/ (or a new sub-package) that lists each origin with its cert count, expandable to show per-cert subject + SKI + which CCADB bucket (or 'not in CCADB') the cert falls into. Reuse TightTwoColLayout for the per-cert detail rows.

Add table-driven tests in internal/resources/ for: distro detection from sample os-release fixtures, anchor-dir enumeration with mixed .crt/.pem files, NSS DB parsing with a stubbed certutil. Use t.Setenv to redirect HOME and t.TempDir() for fixtures.
Document the new Trust Sources tab and per-origin labels in CLAUDE.md and README.md.
acceptance_criteria: On Debian/Ubuntu, certs from /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/ are listed under origin 'distro-anchor-dir' separately from the bulk of /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt.
On RHEL/Fedora, certs from /etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors/ are listed under origin 'distro-anchor-dir'.
If certutil is installed and ~/.pki/nssdb exists, certs from it appear under origin 'nss-user'. If certutil is missing, the section shows a clear note rather than failing.
Trust Sources tab shows totals per origin (e.g., 'system bundle: 148, distro-anchor-dir: 2, nss-user: 0, nss-firefox: 1') and lets the user expand each to inspect individual certs.
Existing 'Compare Local vs CCADB' tab continues to work and now also surfaces the origin label for any cert it shows.
All new code has unit tests; package coverage in internal/resources/ does not regress.
